commit.message:
chore: add .gitleaks.toml to allowlist doc placeholders

The curl-auth-header rule flags the example `curl -H "Authorization:
Bearer YOUR_PASSWORD"` command in docs/troubleshooting.md as a secret.
This is a false positive — YOUR_PASSWORD is a placeholder, not a real
credential. Allowlist the path to suppress the finding.

redirect completes.

Update rollback.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Feature] Add Debian (.deb) packaging support for FE and BE (#68821)

Why I'm doing:
Currently, StarRocks installation on Debian-based systems (Ubuntu/Debian) relies on manual tarball extraction and manual environment configuration. This is error-prone and does not follow the Linux Standard Base (LSB) hierarchy. Providing native .deb packages simplifies deployment ensures consistent directory structures across clusters and enables modern process management via systemd.

What I'm doing:
Introduced a Debian packaging infrastructure for both Frontend (FE) and Backend (BE) components.

Implementation Details:

Standardized Path Mapping:

Binaries & Libraries: Moved to /usr/lib/starrocks/ to keep the application isolated.
Configuration: Relocated to /etc/starrocks/ and registered as conffiles to ensure user settings persist through package upgrades.
Logs: Directed to /var/log/starrocks/.
Metadata/Data: Directed to /var/lib/starrocks/.
Automated Lifecycle Management: * Added a postinst script to automate the creation of a dedicated starrocks system user/group.

Automated directory permission handling (chown/chmod) for log and data paths.
Systemd Integration: * Created starrocks-fe.service and starrocks-be.service units.

Configured services to run as the starrocks user with appropriate resource limits (LimitNOFILE).
Integrated --daemon to allow systemd to track process health correctly.
Build Automation: Provided a cross-platform build.sh script (compatible with macOS/BSD and Linux/GNU sed) to generate the .deb files from the official build output.

Fixes #68205

Signed-off-by: sunghj1118 <sunghj1118@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Hyunjoon Sung <60638615+sunghj1118@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(task): handle CancellationError to prevent crash on navigation (#9)

When navigating back while a `.task` is still running, the task
cancellation throws `CancellationError` which was unhandled, crashing
the app.

### Changes

- **Throwing overloads**: Added `.task` and `.task(id:)` overloads
accepting `@Sendable () async throws -> Void`, matching SwiftUI's API
- **CancellationError handling**: `startTask()` now wraps the action in
`do/catch`, silently ignoring `CancellationError` (and other thrown
errors) — consistent with SwiftUI behavior
- **Tests**: 4 new tests covering cancellation on view removal,
cancellation on ID change, and non-cancellation error handling

### Usage

```swift
// Throwing closures now work directly — CancellationError is caught by the framework
Text("Detail")
  .task(id: itemId) {
    let item = try await api.fetchItem(itemId)
    await MainActor.run { self.item = item }
  }
```

Previously, navigating back during the fetch would crash. Now the
cancellation is silently absorbed.

Rewrite Identity module Blazor pages to use MudBlazor instead of Blazorise

Replace all Blazorise UI components (Card, Modal, TextEdit, Check, Tabs,
AbpExtensibleDataGrid, Badge, etc.) with MudBlazor equivalents (MudCard,
MudDialog, MudTextField, MudCheckBox, MudTabs, MudTable, MudChip, etc.)
in UserManagement, RoleManagement, and RoleNameComponent.

Key changes:
- MudTable with ServerData for server-side pagination/sorting
- MudDialog with own visibility state instead of Blazorise Modal refs
- Override OnCreatedEntityAsync/OnUpdatedEntityAsync to avoid base class
  Blazorise Modal.Hide() calls that would NullRef without bound modals
- Override CloseCreateModalAsync/CloseEditModalAsync for same reason
- Override GetEntitiesAsync to trigger MudTable.ReloadServerData()
- Keep AbpCrudPageBase inheritance for business logic (entity CRUD,
  permissions, error handling, notifications)
- Keep Blazorise usings in _Imports.razor for base class type resolution
- Keep ExtensionProperties component for extensibility support

Fix hardcoded page size assumption in loader

Replace hardcoded 4096-byte page size with dynamic unix.Getpagesize()
in pageDown() and pageUp() functions. This ensures portability to
architectures with different page sizes (e.g., ARM64 with 16KB or 64KB
pages), supporting future aarch64 port (ROADMAP Phase 7.4).

- Use unix.Getpagesize() from golang.org/x/sys/unix (already imported)
- Calculate page mask dynamically instead of hardcoded 4095
- Maintains correct behavior on x86-64 Linux (4KB pages)
